steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what point the text makes about the people at the gathering. Two details carry the answer: (1) each speaker’s “strokes had gone awry” — their presentation of their artistic vision failed; and (2) each “could not grasp” why. So the correct choice must capture both the failure and the speaker’s ignorance of its cause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Each is contemptuous of the other attendees but strives to impress them.’: The text says nothing about contempt; people turn to their neighbors to clarify their own ideas, not to show disdain. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Each fails at presenting a wholly coherent vision of art but does not understand why.’: Matches “strokes had gone awry” (failure) and “could not grasp” (no understanding of why). ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Each becomes entangled in a debate about art, and no one knows how to resolve the debate.’: There is no debate — each person gives a one-way speech, then privately tries to correct themselves. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Each delights in speaking publicly about abstract subjects but detests speaking privately about them.’: The text implies the opposite: public speech fails, and private follow-up is where they seek to clarify. No delight or detestation is mentioned. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
